highlights.text: I gave these out on Friday at 4pm as well, stating: “M y general lean is we can start back-testing on Monday.
I then wrote at the 4pm close yesterday: “ The bull case is that ES can fill this range out, then continue up.
However what we do know is that one of the most common sequences in ES is what I call “elevator down-into-short squeeze”.
After almost every elevator down sell in ES - over the past nearly 100 years - ES sees an equally violent, tradeable snap back rally, triggered by a Failed Breakdown.

I warned about this on Friday at 4pm, when sentiment was as bearish as sentiment can be, stating: “We need to remember one of the core laws of ES, which is that after every “elevator down” sell, comes an equally violent squeeze.
We don’t predict when it will come - it comes whenever we get the Failed Breakdown.” Where do these squeezes take us?
Targets are 6715-23 which tackles the red “megaphone” above yet again.” We hit the 6715-23 macro red megaphone at 9pm last night, then went elevator down to 6605 by 7am this morning.
Those who understand the “elevator down” into short squeeze sequence now knew what to expect from here: Prepare for the squeeze.
I tweeted live at 9:45AM that the 6605 Failed Breakdown would squeeze us up.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a full run down of the both the short setup that preceded Friday’s elevator down sell, and the long setup that triggered the squeezes we saw Sunday and today.

post_title: Dip Buyers Stepped In 2 Days In A Row For SPX. Are Bulls Really Out Of The Woods Though? Oct 15 Plan
